ledger_gap_type: review passage not independently located and no primary locator in ledger
human_t_cell_scope: Polarized human cord-blood T-cell lines plus antigen-specific human Th1 and Th2 clones
proposed_rationale_wording: CXCR3 is a human Th1-associated chemokine receptor and trafficking marker; it is preferentially expressed by polarized Th1 cells and supports responsiveness to CXCR3 ligands, but is not Th1- or CD4-specific.
claim_scope_and_limits: Supports preferential Th1 association and chemotaxis, not lineage identity; CXCR3 is also expressed by activated CD8, NK, and other CXCR3-positive CD4 states.
source_title: Differential expression of chemokine receptors and chemotactic responsiveness of type 1 T helper cells (Th1s) and Th2s.
exact_locator: Full text, Results and Discussion, paragraph beginning 'We generated Th1 and Th2 lines'; Figure 2B (CXCR1-4 Northern blot), Figure 3 (antigen-specific Th1 versus Th2 clones), and Figure 4 (IP-10 chemotaxis).
located_support_summary: CXCR3 expression was selective for polarized human Th1 cells with a reported 6.7-fold Th1-versus-Th2 difference, confirmed in antigen-specific clones; IP-10 induced greater Th1 chemotaxis.
notes: Replaces review-only provenance with a direct human source; Masopust is not used.

ledger_gap_type: review passage not independently located; registry rationale said required for commitment
human_t_cell_scope: Human Th1 and Th2 lines and clones; activation and cytokine polarization of human naive T cells
proposed_rationale_wording: IL12RB2 encodes the signaling IL-12 receptor beta-2 chain, which is selectively enriched in human Th1 versus Th2 cells and is induced during Th1-polarizing activation; do not describe it as required for Th1 commitment on this evidence.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports selective expression, regulation, high-affinity IL-12 binding, and IL-12 responsiveness. It does not establish that IL12RB2 is universally required for human Th1 commitment.
source_title: Selective expression of an interleukin-12 receptor component by human T helper 1 cells.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Selective Expression of the IL-12R beta2 Chain in Th1 Cells'; Figure 3A-B (Th1 versus Th2 lines and clones), Figure 4 (activation and cytokine regulation), and Figure 5 (IL-12 binding).
located_support_summary: IL-12R beta-2 transcripts were selectively expressed in established human Th1 but not Th2 lines and clones; activation induced the transcript, IL-12 enhanced it, and IL-4 suppressed it. High-affinity IL-12 binding was detected in Th1 lines.
notes: Explicitly removes the word required from the proposed rationale; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Ex vivo and stimulated human CD4 T cells, allergen-specific responses, and short-term human Th2 lines from healthy and allergic donors
proposed_rationale_wording: IL4 is a human Th2-associated cytokine frequently coexpressed with IL13; use it as a type-2 cytokine-program component, not as a lineage-exclusive marker or as proof of a master-driver state.
claim_scope_and_limits: Supports expression within human Th2 responses. IL4 is also produced by Tfh and other immune cells, and RNA abundance does not by itself demonstrate a causal differentiation role.
source_title: Hierarchical IL-5 expression defines a subpopulation of highly differentiated human Th2 cells.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'IL-5 expression is restricted to a minority subpopulation of IL-4+, IL-5+, IL-13+ Th2 cells'; Figure 1A-I, especially 1B and 1D-G.
located_support_summary: Across human CD4 systems and donors, IL4 and IL13 were highly coexpressed and each was detected in roughly 70-80% of the operationally defined Th2 response.
notes: Narrows the registry phrase signature cytokine / master driver to an expression-based module rationale; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Ex vivo and stimulated human CD4 T cells, allergen-specific responses, and short-term human Th2 lines from healthy and allergic donors
proposed_rationale_wording: IL5 is a human Th2 effector cytokine enriched in a minority, more highly differentiated Th2 subpopulation; it is not a universal Th2 marker.
claim_scope_and_limits: Supports a differentiated Th2 effector substate. IL5 is expressed by only a minority of Th2 cells in this study and is also produced by non-T-cell type-2 effectors.
source_title: Hierarchical IL-5 expression defines a subpopulation of highly differentiated human Th2 cells.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'IL-5 expression is restricted to a minority subpopulation of IL-4+, IL-5+, IL-13+ Th2 cells'; Figure 1A and 1C-G; Discussion opening paragraph.
located_support_summary: IL5 was confined to about 17-23% of Th2 cells in the main donor groups, while IL5-positive cells usually coexpressed IL4 and IL13 and showed features of greater differentiation.
notes: Adds the key minority-subpopulation limitation; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Ex vivo and stimulated human CD4 T cells, allergen-specific responses, and short-term human Th2 lines from healthy and allergic donors
proposed_rationale_wording: IL13 is a human Th2-associated effector cytokine frequently coexpressed with IL4; use it as a type-2 cytokine-program component, not a lineage-exclusive marker.
claim_scope_and_limits: Supports expression in human Th2 responses. IL13 is shared with ILC2 and other type-2 states and therefore does not establish CD4 or Th2 identity alone.
source_title: Hierarchical IL-5 expression defines a subpopulation of highly differentiated human Th2 cells.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'IL-5 expression is restricted to a minority subpopulation of IL-4+, IL-5+, IL-13+ Th2 cells'; Figure 1A-I, especially 1B and 1D-G.
located_support_summary: IL13 and IL4 were each detected in roughly 70-80% of the operationally defined Th2 response and were commonly coexpressed in human donor and allergen-specific systems.
notes: Replaces review-only support with direct human CD4 data; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Freshly isolated human memory CD3+CD4+CD45RO+ T cells and in-vitro-polarized human Th17 cells from genotyped healthy donors
proposed_rationale_wording: IL23R is a human memory-Th17-associated receptor that marks IL-23 responsiveness and supports Th17 effector signaling; it should not be described as required for the initial differentiation step or as Th17-exclusive.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports IL23R-plus-CCR6 phenotyping and IL-23-dependent IL17A/STAT3 effector signaling. The source explicitly separates effector response from initial Th17 differentiation.
source_title: The IL23R R381Q gene variant protects against immune-mediated diseases by impairing IL-23-induced Th17 effector response in humans.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Study of circulating Th17 cells in IL23R R381Q gene variant carriers', Figure 1A; Results on IL-23-induced effector response, Figure 6A-D; Discussion paragraph beginning 'A key finding'.
located_support_summary: The study defined circulating human Th17 cells as IL-23R+CCR6+ within memory helper T cells and showed that the protective IL23R variant reduced IL-23-induced IL17A production and STAT3 phosphorylation without materially changing Th17 differentiation.
notes: Uses an association-and-function rationale rather than an unqualified maintenance or necessity claim; Masopust is not used.

ledger_gap_type: Acosta-Rodriguez citation did not support CD161; correct source needed
human_t_cell_scope: Human circulating CD4 T-cell clones, ex vivo adult blood CD4 T cells, umbilical-cord and thymic CD4 precursors, and inflamed human tissues
proposed_rationale_wording: KLRB1/CD161 is a human Th17-associated surface marker and precursor feature, but it has low specificity and should not be treated as sufficient for Th17 identity.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports enrichment in human Th17 clones and containment of IL17-producing cells in the CD161-positive fraction in the studied systems. CD161 is broadly present on NK, NKT, MAIT, CD8, and other CD4 populations.
source_title: Human interleukin 17-producing cells originate from a CD161+CD4+ T cell precursor.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Human Th17, but neither Th1 nor Th2, clones express CD161 on their surface', Figure 1A-C; next Results section, Figure 2A-B; Abstract for precursor scope.
located_support_summary: CD161/KLRB1 was strongly enriched in human Th17 clones relative to Th1 and Th2 clones; IL17-producing circulating CD4 cells were found in the CD161-positive fraction, with RORC and IL23R enriched in CD161+CCR6+ cells.
notes: Corrects the ledger miscitation from Acosta-Rodriguez 2007 to Cosmi 2008 and retains the low-specificity warning; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Human tonsillar and peripheral-blood memory T cells, including CD4-enriched CXCR5-positive populations and tonsillar T-cell/B-cell cocultures
proposed_rationale_wording: CXCR5 is a human follicular-homing and B-cell-helper-associated receptor used to mark a Tfh-like program; it is not Tfh- or CD4-specific in mixed-cell data because B cells also express CXCR5.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports follicular localization, chemotaxis, and B-cell helper function of human CXCR5-positive T cells. It does not make CXCR5 sufficient for Tfh identity.
source_title: CXC chemokine receptor 5 expression defines follicular homing T cells with B cell helper function.
exact_locator: Full text, Results sections 'CXCR5+ T Cells in Blood and Tonsils' and 'B Cell Helper Function of CXCR5+ T Cells'; Table I and Figure 3B; follicular localization in Figures 4-5.
located_support_summary: More than 90% of human CXCR5-positive blood T cells were CD4 memory cells; tonsillar CXCR5-positive T cells localized to follicles and induced substantially more IgG and IgA than CXCR5-negative T cells in B-cell coculture.
notes: Primary human source replaces Crotty review-only provenance; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Primary human tonsillar CD4 Tfh and GC-Tfh subsets, with BCL6 transduction of sorted human CD4 T cells
proposed_rationale_wording: BCL6 is a central human Tfh-program transcriptional regulator that is enriched in CXCR5-positive tonsillar Tfh cells and can induce major Tfh migration and T:B-interaction modules; it is not T-cell-specific because germinal-center B cells also express BCL6.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports enrichment and causal induction of a Tfh-like program in human CD4 T cells. It does not support BCL6 as a CD4- or cell-type-exclusive readout.
source_title: Bcl6 and Maf cooperate to instruct human follicular helper CD4 T cell differentiation.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Bcl6 protein is expressed by human Tfh and GC Tfh', Figure 1A-C; section 'Bcl6 instructs the conversion of Tfh to GC Tfh', Figure 2C-D and 2K; Figure 3 for conversion of CXCR5-negative CD4 cells.
located_support_summary: CXCR5-high and CXCR5-intermediate human tonsillar CD4 subsets expressed more BCL6 protein than CXCR5-negative cells; experimental BCL6 expression increased CXCR5 and other migration and T:B-interaction genes in primary human CD4 cells.
notes: Primary human mechanistic source replaces Crotty review-only provenance; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Primary human tonsillar CXCR5-intermediate Tfh and CXCR5-high GC-Tfh CD4 subsets, B-cell coculture, and human CD4 transduction experiments
proposed_rationale_wording: IL21 is a prominent human Tfh/GC-Tfh helper cytokine and supports a Tfh-like effector program, but it is shared with other CD4 and innate-like lymphocyte states and is not sufficient for Tfh identity.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports production by human tonsillar Tfh populations and IL21-dependent B-cell help. The same article shows that BCL6 alone is not sufficient to induce IL21 and that Maf can enhance it.
source_title: Bcl6 and Maf cooperate to instruct human follicular helper CD4 T cell differentiation.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Bcl6 protein is expressed by human Tfh and GC Tfh', Figure 1D (IL21-dependent IgM help); section 'Helper cytokines IL-4 and IL-21', Figure 5; section 'Maf induces IL-21 secretion', Figure 7B-C.
located_support_summary: Human CXCR5-positive tonsillar Tfh subsets provided largely IL21-dependent B-cell help; GC-Tfh cells produced IL21, frequently with IL4, and Maf transduction increased IL21 production in primary human CD4 cells.
notes: Primary human source replaces Crotty review-only provenance while retaining cross-program sharing; Masopust is not used.

ledger_gap_type: Thornton supported thymic-origin hypothesis, not stability; human activation counterevidence needed
human_t_cell_scope: Human PBMC CD4 T cells, human CD4+FOXP3+ Tregs, human CD4 and CD8 responder cells, plus parallel murine experiments
proposed_rationale_wording: IKZF2/Helios is enriched in activated human Tregs but is also induced by T-cell activation and proliferation; do not call it a Treg-stability marker or a reliable thymic-origin discriminator.
claim_scope_and_limits: This human primary study directly undercuts stability and origin-specific wording. Helios can remain a bounded, activation-overlapping Treg-associated module component, not a lineage-stability claim.
source_title: Helios expression is a marker of T cell activation and proliferation.
exact_locator: Abstract; Results Figure 1B (human Treg-associated markers), Figure 3E-F and Figure 4 (activation/division in human responders), Figure 6B (CD3/CD28 plus or minus IL2), and Discussion conclusion.
located_support_summary: Helios was enriched with human Treg-associated markers but was induced in activated and dividing human CD4 and CD8 cells and declined after resting; the authors concluded it was unsuitable for distinguishing natural from induced Tregs.
notes: Thornton 2010 may remain only as the original thymic-origin hypothesis; neither Thornton nor Masopust supports stability.

human_t_cell_scope: Freshly isolated and activated human peripheral-blood CD4+CD25+ cells with measured anergy and contact-dependent suppressive activity
proposed_rationale_wording: CTLA4 is enriched intracellularly and persists after activation in suppressive human CD4+CD25+ regulatory cells; treat it as a Treg-associated checkpoint with substantial activation overlap, not as a Treg-specific identity marker or a proven sole effector mechanism.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports expression in a functionally regulatory human population. In this study, CTLA4 blockade did not reverse suppression, so the source should not be stretched to prove that CTLA4 alone mediates suppressive function.
source_title: Identification and functional characterization of human CD4(+)CD25(+) T cells with regulatory properties isolated from peripheral blood.
exact_locator: Full text, Results paragraph immediately before Figure 2 and Figure 2A-B; suppression and blocking experiments in Figures 4 and 6; Discussion paragraph on CTLA4.
located_support_summary: A CD45RO-positive subset of freshly isolated human CD4+CD25+ cells contained intracellular CTLA4 and displayed prolonged surface CTLA4 after activation while showing anergy and suppression. Blocking results did not establish CTLA4 as the operative suppressive mechanism.
notes: Replaces uncited association with direct human evidence and weakens the registry's effector-molecule implication; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: FOXP3+ Tregs and conventional CD4 T cells from treatment-naive human breast tumors, normal breast tissue, blood, and several additional solid tumors
proposed_rationale_wording: CCR8 marks an activated tissue/tumor-resident human Treg state and is enriched over conventional CD4 T cells in multiple tumors; it is context-dependent and should not be treated as a universal peripheral-Treg marker.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports a tissue/tumor Treg association, functional CCL1 chemotaxis, and activation context. The article also identifies NKT expression and much lower peripheral-blood Treg expression.
source_title: Regulatory T Cells Exhibit Distinct Features in Human Breast Cancer.
exact_locator: Full text, Results sections 'CCR8 is Expressed by Intratumoral Treg Cells' and preceding CCR8 differential-expression paragraph; Figure 6A-D (cell-type and tumor comparisons), Figure 6E (CCL1 chemotaxis), and Figure 7B-E (activation/proliferation context).
located_support_summary: CCR8 was strongly enriched on human tumor-resident FOXP3+ Tregs relative to Tconv cells across breast and other solid tumors, supported by more than 70 breast-cancer samples; CCR8-positive tumor Tregs migrated toward CCL1.
notes: Narrows tissue/effector wording to the human tumor/tissue context actually tested; Masopust is not used.

human_t_cell_scope: Cloned human peripheral-blood CD25+CD4+ T cells functionally classified as suppressive or nonsuppressive
proposed_rationale_wording: TNFRSF18/GITR is associated with suppressive human CD25+CD4+ T-cell clones, but it is also activation-induced on conventional effector T cells; use it as a Treg-associated costimulatory-receptor component, not a Treg-specific marker or proof of suppressive necessity.
claim_scope_and_limits: Directly supports higher expression and correlation with suppressive capacity. The same source found no reversal with anti-GITR or recombinant ligand and explicitly states that GITR is not specific because activated effector CD4 cells express it.
source_title: Human CD25+CD4+ T suppressor cell clones produce transforming growth factor beta, but not interleukin 10, and are distinct from type 1 T regulatory cells.
exact_locator: Full text, Results section 'Expression of CTLA-4 and GITR on CD25+CD4+ T Cell Clones', Figure 6 and the following functional paragraph; Discussion paragraph beginning 'Suppressive and nonsuppressive clones'.
located_support_summary: Suppressive human CD25+CD4+ clones expressed four- to sixfold more GITR than nonsuppressive clones, with a significant expression-function correlation; perturbation did not establish a causal suppressive role, and activated effector expression limits specificity.
notes: Closes the uncited gap with a source that contains its own activation/specificity caveat; Masopust is not used.
